zh.Piscataway Parish,Charles,Maryland: 美國馬里蘭州查爾斯比斯卡塔瓦 帕里什
zh.Prince George's, Maryland, British Colon: 布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加马里兰普林斯 格奧智也斯
zh.lifeSummary: 當John Mason於1693年7月19日出生在美國馬里蘭州查爾斯比斯卡塔瓦 帕里什時，他的父親Philip Mason32歲，母親Mary Pigman24歲。他至少有1個兒子。他於1693年住在美國馬里蘭州查爾斯。他去世於1755年享年62歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John Mason于1693年7月19日出生在美国马里兰州查爾斯比斯卡塔瓦 帕里什时，他的父亲Philip Mason32岁，母亲Mary Pigman24岁。他至少有1个儿子。他于1693年住在美国马里兰州查爾斯。他去世于1755年享年62岁。

nameMeanings.name: Mason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English masoun ‘mason, stone worker, builder in stone’ (Old Central French maçon, masson). A Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun) gives rise to Machin and its variants. See also Mayson . Stonemasonry was a hugely important craft in the Middle Ages.

variants.name: Masson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Lucien, Alain, Henri, Pierre, Yves, Agathe, Aime, Cecile, Clovis, Francois, Francoise.

variants.name: Machin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Juan, Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Mario, Miguel, Pedro, Blanca, Diego, Luis, Ramon.
English: variant of   Machen  . This is the more common form of the name in Britain and Ireland.
Spanish (Machín): probably a nickname from machín ‘boor, lout’, often applied to a blacksmith's apprentice.

variants.name: Macon
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places so called, e.g. in Saône-et-Loire.
French: from the old oblique form of the ancient Germanic personal name Mako, a variant of Mago, a short form of compound names formed with maht ‘strength, power’ as the first element.
French (Maçon): occupational name for a mason, (Old) French maçon.

variants.name: Machen
meanings: English: variant of Mason , from the Middle English form machun (derived from Old French machun).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When John Mason was born on 19 July 1693, in Piscataway Parish, Charles, Maryland, United States, his father, Philip Mason, was 32 and his mother, Mary Pigman, was 24. He had at least 1 son. He lived in Charles, Maryland, United States in 1693. He died in 1755, at the age of 62.

ja.lifeSummary: John Masonが1693年7月19日にアメリカ合衆国メリーランド州チャーレスピスカタワイ パーイッシュで生まれた時，父Philip Masonは32歳，母Mary Pigmanは24歳であった。彼は少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1693年にはアメリカ合衆国メリーランド州チャーレスに住んでいた。彼は1755年に62歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: John Mason(이)가 1693년 7월 19일 미국 메릴랜드 주 찰스 피스카타웨이 파리시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Philip Mason(은)는 32세였고, 그의 어머니 Mary Pigman(은)는 24세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 1693년도에 미국 메릴랜드 주 찰스에서 살았다. 그는 1755년 62세의 나이로 사망했다.
